name: Brita Style Essential Water Filter Pitcher
pros: Complete starter kit at ¥5,104 — entry price for clean drinking water is unbeatable
Compact 2.4L pitcher fits easily into Japanese apartment fridges and cabinets
Comes with one Maxtra Pro cartridge; nearly 1,600 positive reviews confirm durability
cons: Plastic build quality feels basic; not as sleek as premium Brita models
Filtered capacity of 1.26L means frequent refilling for households with multiple people
shortDescription: Affordable Brita pitcher starter set with integrated filter for improving tap water taste.
review: For expats new to Japan wanting cleaner-tasting tap water without complexity, this is an easy win. At ¥5,104, it's cheaper than many reusable water bottles, and Brita's Maxtra Pro cartridge handles chlorine and mineral taste well. The pitcher is compact enough for Tokyo apartments. Just note the filtered capacity is modest (1.26L) — better for 1-2 people than large families.
